_meta.note: Parameters based on bird behavior observation. Adjust to actual flock response. Whole-house brooding assumed.
_meta.ventilation_note: Minimum ventilation to maintain temperature, remove CO2/NH3, supply fresh air. Increase as birds grow.

vent_temperature_target.note: Cloacal/vent temperature — assess with thermometer + behavior observation

weekly_environment._description: Target environment by bird weight (g) — adjust based on behavior, not age alone

by_week._description: Approximate weekly environment targets for broiler. Adjust based on bird behavior.

lighting_notes.day_1_7: 23 h light / 1 h dark. Intensity 30–40 lux. Gradually increase darkness to 4–6 h by day 7.
lighting_notes.day_7_plus: Min 4 h darkness per night (continuous block). Intensity 5–10 lux.
lighting_notes.pre_catch: 23 h light / 1 h dark for 3 days before catching. Reduce intensity during catching.

minimum.purpose: Remove CO2, NH3, moisture. Maintain temperature.
minimum.note: Minimum rate must always be maintained regardless of temperature.

transitional.note: Increase inlet openings as bird age and heat production grows.

tunnel.note: Close side inlets, use tunnel fans. Effective wind-chill cools birds above 20°C (68°F).

ammonia_targets.note: Ammonia above 25 ppm causes respiratory irritation. Check at bird level, not standing height.

co2_targets.note: CO2 above 3000 ppm indicates inadequate ventilation.

stocking_density.note: Comply with local regulations. Welfare certifications may set lower limits (e.g., 30 kg/m²).
